mission.title: knative: Feature Request: Support Downward API
mission.description: Feature Request: Support Downward API. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current knative deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
helm list -n knative 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative installation.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## In what area(s)?

## Describe the feature

There are many use-cases for knowing information about a particular revision from within the user container.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feature Request: Support Downward API
steps.description:
Going to leave this open & in the icebox for now

Further areas to round out support could include:
1) Support Downward API properties through volumes
2) Potentially allow some non-leaky properties through without the need for the flag to be turned on - ie. `metadata.*`

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feature Request: Support Downward API" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Going to leave this open & in the icebox for now

Further areas to round out support could include:
1) Support Downward API properties through volumes
2) Potentially allow some non-leaky properties through without the need for the flag to be turned on - ie. `metadata.*`

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
